contexts.context: ...admitted, he is constantly admonished to choose some <mark>loftier</mark> theme, as if any theme could have been...
...—a deeper looking into man's heart and destiny—and <mark>loftier</mark> aspirations after all "that is very far off," than...
...lit my being, though its raceMight have been <mark>loftier</mark> far.    .    .    .    ..    .    .    .    .    .    For thee alone, for thee!...
